mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 50+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of autos and light trucks combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending without triggering monetary tightening, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-recession recovery phases reflects improving capacity utilization and corporate profitability, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery regimes, or the mechanism Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 700,000 units signals a contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST drops below 700.0, or the signal exhibits negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strengthening, which supports consumer confidence and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when adjusted for seasonal employment trends.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of central bank tightening and higher discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal predictive power disappears after controlling for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling validation windows.
